query: NATO summit 2026 5% GDP defense spending target by 2035 declaration text member commitments

{"query":"NATO summit 2026 5% GDP defense spending target by 2035 declaration text member commitments","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1321,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.congress.gov/crs-product/R49018","title":"NATO: Issues for the July 2026 Ankara Summit | Congress.gov | Library of Congress","content":"At NATO's June 2025 summit in The Hague, the Netherlands, the allies adopted a new defense investment plan under which they agreed to "invest 5% of GDP [i.e., gross domestic product] annually on core defense requirements as well as defense- and security-related spending by 2035." The 5% commitment is to encompass 3.5% of GDP for "core defense requirements" and 1.5% of GDP for defense-related expenditures, including building relevant infrastructure and defense industrial capacity.
In 2014, the allies adopted the so-called Wales Defense Investment Pledge, under which they committed to "aim to move towards" adopting national defense budgets that meet or exceed 2% of GDP, and devote at least 20% of defense spending to procurement and modernization, by the year 2024.23 NATO estimates published in March 2026 indicated that all NATO members met the 2%-of-GDP benchmark in 2025, compared with three allies in 2014, and that all allies met the 20% procurement and modernization target, compared with eight allies in 2014.24
He added that Germany, with Europe's largest defense budget (about $120 billion in 2025), has doubled its defense budget since 2022 and has concrete plans to reach the 3.5% of GDP core defense spending target in 2029.26 Secretary General Rutte also has acknowledged reports suggesting that some allies—including the Czech Republic, Hungary, and Slovenia—may fall below 2% of GDP defense spending this year, and that others—including Spain, Italy, and the UK—do not have plans in place to meet the new spending targets agreed at last year's summit.27"},{"url":"https://www.nato.int/en/about-us/official-texts-and-resources/official-texts/2026/07/08/the-ankara-summit-declaration","title":"The Ankara Summit Declaration | NATO Official text","content":"For 2026, Allies pledge €70 billion in military equipment, assistance and training for Ukraine and affirm their sovereign commitments to sustaining at least equivalent levels in 2027."},{"url":"https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Agreement_on_5%25_NATO_defence_spending_by_2035","title":"Agreement on 5% NATO defence spending by 2035 - Wikipedia","content":"At the 2025 The Hague NATO summit, member states pledged to increase defence spending to five percent of GDP in what is called The Hague Investment Plan.
Under this declaration, all 32 NATO member states except Spain, which received an exemption, committed to raising their annual defence and security-related expenditures to 5% of gross domestic product (GDP) by 2035, with progress to be reviewed in 2029.
Similarly, as of June 13, 2025, the Dutch caretaker cabinet has decided to increase the defence budget to 3.5% of the gross domestic product.
Prior to the summit, Poland and the Baltic states, already approaching or exceeding 4%, championed ambitious targets, framing higher spending as essential deterrence.
Spain subsequently agreed to be excluded from the 5% requirement in the final summit text, although the declaration still stipulates the 2035 timeline and 2029 review date.
While Eastern European nations and the UK broadly support the 5% escalation, Spain argues that its defense capabilities—backed by personnel, deployments, and industry—are achievable without a fixed GDP percentage."},{"url":"https://www.nato.int/en/what-we-do/introduction-to-nato/defence-expenditures-and-natos-5-commitment","title":"Defence investment and NATO's 5% commitment","content":"Jun 29, 2026 — The 5% Hague defence commitment. At the 2025 NATO Summit in The Hague, Allies committed to investing 5% of GDP annually on defence by 2035.Read more"},{"url":"https://www.washingtonpost.com/world/2026/07/06/nato-summit-spending-rutte/11b44da6-7943-11f1-b194-f872dd4ec5aa_story.html","title":"NATO chief demands allies present credible plans to reach ...","content":"Jul 6, 2026 — The 32 nations agreed last year to invest 5% of their gross domestic product on defense — 3.5% on their defense budgets and 1.5% on roads, ...Read more"}]}

At the 2025 The Hague NATO summit, member states pledged to increase defence spending to five percent of GDP in what is called The Hague Investment Plan. The Agreement on 5% Defence Spending by 2035 was the principal outcome of the 2025 NATO Summit, held in The Hague, Netherlands, on 24–25 June 2025. Under this declaration, all 32 NATO member states except Spain, which received an exemption, committed to raising their annual defence and security-related expenditures to 5% of gross domestic product (GDP) by 2035, with progress to be reviewed in 2029.","score":0.90633047,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.reuters.com/world/americas/five-nato-members-seen-spend-over-35-gdp-core-defence-this-year-alliance-2026-07-07","title":"Five NATO members seen to spend over 3.5% of GDP on core defence this year, alliance estimates show","content":"Exclusive news, data and analytics for financial market professionals Learn more about Refinitiv. ## Browse World. *   Ukraine and Russia at War. ## Browse Business. ANKARA, July 7 (Reuters) - Five NATO members are projected to meet the alliance's goal of spending 3.5% of gross domestic product on core defence ​already in 2026, according to updated NATO data published ‌on Tuesday ahead of a leaders' summit in Ankara, which also showed some members are still expected to spend only about 2%. At a summit in The ​Hague last year, NATO leaders pledged to spend 3.5% of ​GDP on core defence items such as weapons and ⁠troops by 2035 - up from a previous goal of 2%. In total, European NATO members and Canada are projected to spend 2.53% of GDP ​this year on core defence. *   #### World-Check, opens new tab. *   About Reuters, opens new tab. *   Media Center, opens new tab. *   Reuters News Agency, opens new tab.","score":0.80844593,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/amp/2025/06/25/nato-allies-agree-to-higher-5percent-defense-spending-target.html","title":"NATO allies agree to higher 5% defense spending target","content":"* The NATO alliance agreed to increase defense spending from 2% to 5% of gross domestic product, by 2035. * The alliance on Wednesday also reaffirmed its \"ironclad commitment to collective defence\" as enshrined in Article 5. Mark Rutte, secretary general of the North Atlantic Treaty Organization (NATO), left, and U.S. President Donald Trump, during the NATO summit in The Hague, Netherlands, on Wednesday, June 25, 2025. NATO allies on Wednesday agreed to more than double their defense spending target from 2% of gross domestic product to 5% by 2035, in the most decisive move from the alliance in more than a decade. The alliance on Wednesday also reaffirmed its \"ironclad commitment to collective defence\" as enshrined in Article 5 that an attack on one is an attack on all, following question marks over U.S. reliability when it came to that central pillar of NATO.","score":0.7969415,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.semafor.com/article/06/22/2025/nato-agrees-on-5-defense-spending-target","title":"NATO agrees on 5% defense spending target","content":"NATO members agreed Sunday to increase their national defense spending to 5% of GDP by 2035, a benchmark US President Donald Trump has long advocated for.","score":0.7880784,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.csis.org/analysis/what-does-nato-defense-spending-look-heading-ankara-summit","title":"What Does NATO Defense Spending Look Like Heading into the Ankara ...","content":"NATO heads of state committed that their governments would spend a total of 5 percent of GDP on defense and security activities by 2035.","score":0.75049835,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":0.7,"request_id":"b5ab0bc1-8cc6-4145-babe-dd2398de15b9"}
